title: Real Operation Yashima
wikitext:
[]: Real Operation Yashima             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Sat Mar 12, 2011 4:36 am [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2011-03-12/evangelion-inspires-real-operation-yashima-after-quake[/url] I couldn't help but thinking about Evangelion when hearing about the devastating events in Japan, and apparently, neither could this company. I hope their plan works.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Sat Mar 12, 2011 4:50 am That's simply too awesome for words. Just goes to show you how influential Eva is in Japan. Go Operation Yashima!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Mar 12, 2011 5:12 am Awesome nuff said

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Mar 12, 2011 6:01 am ANN isn´t working for me, fuck.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Sat Mar 12, 2011 6:16 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: ANN isn´t working for me, fuck.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Mar 12, 2011 8:26 am Awesome news and hope it worked out! It's almost 10:30 PM over there. My inner otaku is warm and fuzzy right now. (Seriously, I knew Eva was huge in Japan, but I didn't know it was THAT huge.) So that reactor didn't cause too much damage in the end, despite 4 hurt people? I hope everybody in Japan stays safe! No more lost life or damage!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 12, 2011 9:25 am LOL, them Otakus are everywhere. Humor is always an effective way to keep cool in difficult situations.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Mar 12, 2011 12:58 pm That's pretty awesome. Let's hope the plan works!

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Mar 12, 2011 2:13 pm The one bright spot in all this madness.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sat Mar 12, 2011 2:21 pm Awesome! Has anyone been able to translate the "Nerv bulletin" that the page links to? It seems to be a image file, so Google Translate isn't catching it.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Mar 12, 2011 2:48 pm AWESOME! (and it's not even 2015!) ... though, I would like to give a moment of silence for those who were in the earthquake. ... now that that's over with, GO, EVA YASHIMA!

[]:             Quote Post by Xero759 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 4:50 am Too cool for words. Is it weird that I want a T-Shirt of one of the logos?

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 5:26 am Xero759 wrote: Too cool for words. Is it weird that I want a T-Shirt of one of the logos?

[]:             Quote Post by LoCooLaidman » Tue Mar 15, 2011 12:58 pm Ah, no one would understand this but the Evageeks... best wishes!

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Wed Mar 16, 2011 11:19 am Well, it didn't took long for Kotono Mitsuishi -- ol' Misato-sensei -- to take notice of the ongoing Operation: http://kotochawan.com/main/?itemid=1221 http://kotochawan.com/main/?itemid=1222 I think she's really impressed with the efforts made to conserve electricity... and who knows, she might as well bother Khara to support the whole effort altogether.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 16, 2011 12:20 pm Ogata noticed this as well, she's been urging everybody in her own blog for people to keep at it (and even doing some Shinji "character-speak"): http://emou.seesaa.net/article/190255400.html http://emou.seesaa.net/article/190676266.html Mitsuishi on the other hand, has said "of course, I approve this" (kinda in a Misato-ish way); both have said they hope everybody is safe and to just keep at it with helping.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Mar 17, 2011 11:27 am Apparently, it's working. They expected a blackout in Tokyo for Today, but there was none. There's not enough power for trains to run, but greater catastrophes were averted, at least as far as the power grid is concerned. BTW *looks at the picture in the blog* This beauty does Shinji's voice?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Mar 17, 2011 11:30 am Kendrix wrote: Apparently, it's working. They expected a blackout in Tokyo for Today, but there was none. There's not enough power for trains to run, but greater catastrophes were averted, at least as far as the power grid is concerned. BTW *looks at the picture in the blog* This beauty does Shinji's voice?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Mar 17, 2011 11:56 am soul.assassin wrote: That's right -- Ogata's the legendary "lady killer". ;)
summary:
[]: Real Operation Yashima             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Sat Mar 12, 2011 4:36 am [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2011-03-12/evangelion-inspires-real-operation-yashima-after-quake[/url] I couldn't help but thinking about Evangelion when hearing about the devastating events in Japan, and apparently, neither could this company. I hope their plan works.
